economic_notes: Bonaparte First Nation has investments in 1049280 BC Ltd. and Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership (GBEs accounted for by modified equity method). Natural Resources and Economic Development is a major program segment. Nation subject to undetermined legal claims. Assets damaged in 2018 natural disaster with no assessment completed.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to management not completing assessment of tangible capital assets for PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations — impact on expenses, surplus, TCA and accumulated surplus is unquantified for both 2023 and 2022. Fiscal year is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Bonaparte Social Housing is fully consolidated subsidiary. 1049280 BC Ltd. and Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership are GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Net financial assets on balance sheet shown as 5,402,967 but Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets shows 5,447,740 — slight discrepancy in statements. Total government transfers estimated as ISC 5,563,751 + ISC recoveries 346,480 + FNHA 759,180 + Government of Canada 786,964 + CMHC 40,000 + Other funding sources 1,082,629 + Province of BC 2,969,387 = 11,548,391 (approximately; some line items may include non-government amounts). Accumulated surplus per Note 10 is 21,118,753 vs balance sheet 21,229,316 — discrepancy noted. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Capital expenses segment 990,890 included under expenses_infrastructure is ambiguous; Operations & Maintenance used for infrastructure.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
